method: Manual full-codebase review (Bugbot + Security subagents had no git diff on main); UX/UI + color audit via admin screenshots and component/token review (2026-06-11)

bugbot.finding: submitForm accepts arbitrary answers with no server-side validation against the form definition (required fields, types).
Submission and lead inserts are not wrapped in a transaction; partial failure can leave orphaned rows.
getFormStats loads all submissions, leads, and analytics events via Drizzle with: — unbounded memory as forms grow.
slugify() can return an empty string for titles with only special characters.
Analytics useEffect fires form_viewed / form_started on pageIndex changes, inflating funnel metrics.
Multi-page forms do not validate required fields before advancing pages.
listLeads without formId fetches latest 200 leads globally then filters in memory — can miss older user leads.

security.finding: Public submitForm has no rate limiting or CAPTCHA — open to submission spam and DB flooding.
Public trackEvent has no rate limiting — easy to inflate analytics or bloat analytics_events.
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET is optional in env schema; misconfigured deploy could run without a proper secret.
No size limits on answers / metadata JSON blobs — large payloads enable storage abuse.
OpenAPI docs at /api are public — exposes full API surface if internet-facing.
Inngest handler is public; ensure INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY is set in production.

ux.finding: Builder live preview is a separate static Preview component (pointer-events-none fake inputs), not FormRenderer — diverges on progress bar, validation, page transitions, and field chrome. Staff cannot trust WYSIWYG.
Progress bar uses page index only (e.g. single-step forms show Step 1 of 1 and 100% before any answers). Misleading for lead-capture and funnel analytics.

Checkbox fields use Question + Placeholder in inspector but render placeholder ?? label in preview/renderer; validation errors reference field.label. Confusing for builders (e.g. label 'valid?' vs type 'Checkbox').
View live link uses server form.slug while editor allows unsaved local slug edits — opens wrong URL if user changed slug without saving.
Unsaved changes guarded only via beforeunload — no in-app blocker when navigating sidebar, back link, or Build/Results tabs. Risk of silent data loss.
Create form mutation has no onError / toast — duplicate slug and server errors fail silently (unlike editor save/publish).
Each form row wrapped in full double-bezel Panel — heavy visual weight; list feels flat and card-heavy on ivory canvas.
Form rows only offer Edit and Open live — no duplicate, archive, delete, or copy-link actions.
Draft forms have no Open live or share preview from list; only builder preview exists until publish.
Loading states are plain text strings only — no skeletons; layout shifts when data arrives.
No search or sort on forms list or leads list — will not scale beyond small datasets (leads capped at 200 server-side).
Sidebar brand link targets marketing home (/) instead of admin workspace (/admin) for logged-in staff.
Sidebar account area shows avatar + Sign out only — no display name or email for identity on shared machines.
Drag handle and delete control opacity-0 until group-hover — poor discoverability for non-power users.
Field inspector and step settings live below structure rail in one scroll column — settings scroll out of view as forms grow.
Save control shows disabled outline 'Saved' when clean — reads as broken/inactive rather than success state (see also COLOR-011).
Edit JSON toggle sits in tab row without advanced affordance or warning — easy mis-click for non-technical builders.
Publish allowed without inline summary of prerequisites (empty steps, missing required fields, etc.).
Results funnel lists raw event_type strings (form_viewed, field_answered) — not human-readable for marketing/sales staff.
Results tab uses large hero metric row (Views/Starts/Completions) — contradicts PRODUCT.md anti-reference for generic SaaS dashboard patterns.
Lead status updates have no success/error toast — failures are silent.
Native HTML select for lead status breaks rounded Input/Button design language.
Leads list only — no detail view, notes, owner assignment, or drill-down (see backlog lead-detail-notes).
Submissions table shows at most 4 field columns plus '+N more'; no pagination UI despite list cap.
Unpublished/missing forms show generic Unavailable message — no staff-facing preview URL for drafts.
No GDPR/consent checkbox or privacy notice in form definition or public renderer despite PII + UTM collection.
Thank-you screen uses font-display (Fraunces); builder ending preview uses system sans — another preview mismatch.
Field reorder drag handle has tabIndex={-1} — not keyboard accessible for inclusive admin use.
Leads pipeline disconnected from per-form Results — no 'view leads for this form' from editor; submissions only linked from Results tab.
PRODUCT.md documents bone background and one sans family; code uses summit-ivory and Fraunces + Plus Jakarta — doc/code drift.
TanStack devtools render bottom-right in development — clutters screenshots and local demos (not production).
Three competing primary accents (mauve, everest-green, gold) with no documented hierarchy — Create, Submit, Publish, Continue use different colors per screen.
No warning when editing slug on published form — breaks shared /f/old-slug links (see backlog slug-change-redirect).
Builder preview select always shows first option; no interactive validation or page transitions — documented gap still open.
Selected field in preview lacks aria-selected; preview fields use role=button wrappers only.

color.finding: Inactive nav indicator uses bg-mauve-20 — ~1.48:1 contrast on white, effectively invisible.
Brand sublabel 'EVEREST FINANCE' uses text-everest-green-60 at 10px uppercase — ~4.18:1 on white, fails WCAG AA normal text (needs 4.5:1).

Active nav uses full mauve pill + white text + shadow; inactive uses text-night-60 only (~5.25:1). Passes AA numerically but inactive items read as disabled, not available — strong perceptual contrast imbalance.
Mobile admin header uses ghost buttons only — no active-state pill or underline; users cannot see current section.
--sidebar-primary, --sidebar-accent, etc. are defined in CSS but AppShell nav uses ad-hoc mauve classes — semantic sidebar tokens unused.
CSS sets --sidebar-primary to everest-green but active nav fill is mauve — primary color role inconsistent across admin shell and actions (links, slugs, outline buttons use green).
Label component uses text-mauve-60 at 11px uppercase — ~4.0:1 on white, fails WCAG AA normal text. Affects builder inspector, table headers, leads labels.
Input placeholders use text-night-40 — ~2.71:1 on white, fails WCAG.
Builder chrome icons use text-mauve-40 — ~2.33:1 on white, fails WCAG.
Structural hairline borders at mauve 10–15% alpha are ~1.2–1.3:1 vs white — boundaries barely visible; fields and panels blend into ivory canvas.
Disabled button variant applies opacity-50 — 'Saved' state at ~2:1 effective contrast reads as broken UI, not success.
Panel double-bezel outer shell (bg-mauve-05 + ring-mauve-10) has very low separation on summit-ivory — forms list lacks depth hierarchy.
11px uppercase kickers and progress labels use text-mauve-60 — borderline or failing at ~4.0:1 on white/ivory.
Active nav gold dot on mauve pill is ~5.23:1 — passes AA for UI components but marginal at 6px; white/ivory dot would be clearer.
Muted text relies on rgba opacity on white rather than solid verified hex tokens — same -60 steps used at sizes requiring 4.5:1 across admin UI.
Preview placeholder and empty-state copy use text-night-40 — fails contrast (~2.71:1).
Unselected field type glyphs use text-mauve-60 on bg-mauve-05 — ~3.65:1, fails normal text AA.
Selected vs unselected rows in structure rail both use subtle mauve-05 washes — selection state easy to miss (luminance too close).

positive: Admin oRPC routes use authedContext with session checks
Form ownership verified on update, publish, and stats (createdBy)
Lead status updates check form ownership
Auth isolated in src/server/auth.ts (avoids pg leaking to client bundle)
Public forms only return published forms by slug
Admin shell layout is coherent: sidebar + workspace, warm ivory canvas, mauve accent
Build vs Results tab separation matches PRODUCT.md strategic principles
Editor dirty state (gold dot), save toasts, and beforeunload guard implemented
Form slug in mono everest-green is strong wayfinding (~14:1 on ivory)
Active nav white-on-mauve and outline everest-green buttons pass AAA contrast
Badge text on tinted backgrounds (mauve/everest at 10% alpha) passes contrast
priority_fixes: Rate limiting on public submit and track endpoints
DB transaction for submission + lead creation
Fix progress bar semantics for single- and multi-step forms (UX-002)
Sidebar nav contrast: inactive dot, sublabel, hover surfaces, mobile active state (COLOR-001–004)
Replace failing muted tokens: labels, placeholders, borders (COLOR-007–011)
Document and unify primary action color (mauve vs everest-green) (COLOR-006, UX-032)
View live + slug edit consistency and published slug warning (UX-004, UX-033)
